content.problemStatement:
An array BBB is called good if the prefix XOR array is non-decreasing. Formally, the following condition should hold:


For all 1≤i<j≤∣B∣1 \le i < j \le |B|1≤i<j≤∣B∣, B1⊕B2⊕....⊕Bi≤B1⊕B2⊕...⊕BjB_1 \oplus B_2 \oplus .... \oplus B_i \le B_1 \oplus B_2 \oplus ... \oplus B_jB1​⊕B2​⊕....⊕Bi​≤B1​⊕B2​⊕...⊕Bj​.
You are given an array AAA with NNN integers, and need to answer QQQ queries of the following form:


Given LLL and RRR, is A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] good where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subarray [AL,AL+1,...,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, ..., A_R][AL​,AL+1​,...,AR​].

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and QQQ, the size of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.
The next QQQ lines contain 222 integers each - LLL and RRR, representing a query.




Output Format
For each test case, output a binary string SSS of length QQQ, where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 if the subarray in the iii-th query is good, else Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Here is the explanation for the queries:

Query 111 : The array is [1,2,5][1, 2, 5][1,2,5]. The prefix XORs are [1,3,6][1, 3, 6][1,3,6]. This is non-decreasing, and so the array is good.
Query 222 : The array is [1,2,5,3][1, 2, 5, 3][1,2,5,3]. The prefix XORs are [1,3,6,5][1, 3, 6, 5][1,3,6,5]. This is not non-decreasing, and so the array is not good.
